title: Compute on event time, not processing time
description: Window and aggregate by the event's own timestamp so results are deterministic regardless of when records arrive or are reprocessed.
body: Use when aggregations must be correct under delays and replays. Do not window on processing time when events can arrive late or out of order — the same input produces different results on replay and during catch-up. Actions: extract the event timestamp, assign watermarks, and key windows on event time so a backfill reproduces live results. Validation signals include identical aggregates between a live run and a replay of the same events, and windows whose contents match the event timestamps. Risk boundary: processing-time windows make a reprocessed pipeline disagree with the original; if event time is unavailable, escalate to source the timestamp before building time-based logic.
